feature_name: Network Security Group Support
notes: Network rule set default_action=Deny → network-level control PASS; SB not subnet-injected so NSG proxy via rule set

feature_name: Virtual Network Integration
notes: Premium-only; non-Premium → UNKNOWN; Premium without VNet rules or private endpoints → FAIL

feature_name: Azure Private Link
notes: At least one Approved private endpoint connection embedded in namespace object → PASS

feature_name: Disable Public Network Access
notes: PASS if public_network_access=Disabled OR network rule set default_action=Deny

feature_name: Monitor Anomalies and Threats Targeting Sensitive Data
notes: public_network_access=Disabled → exfil surface restricted → PASS; no native DLP product for SB message bodies

feature_name: Encrypt Data in Transit
notes: minimum_tls_version in (1.2 / 1.3) → PASS

feature_name: Encrypt Data at Rest with Platform-Managed Keys
notes: All SKUs (Basic/Standard/Premium) encrypted with MS-managed keys by default — auto-PASS (static)

feature_name: Encrypt Data at Rest with Customer-Managed Key
notes: Premium SKU only; encryption.key_source=Microsoft.KeyVault → PASS; non-Premium → UNKNOWN; Premium without CMK → FAIL

feature_name: Manage Cryptographic Keys using Key Management Service
notes: Premium SKU uses KV for CMK key management; ARM-checkable; non-Premium → UNKNOWN

feature_name: Certificate Management using Azure Key Vault
notes: SB TLS certs are Microsoft-managed infrastructure; customer cert management via KV not ARM-readable → UNKNOWN

feature_name: Use Centralized Identity and Authentication System — Disable Local Auth
notes: disable_local_auth=True disables SAS keys; GA property analogous to Redis disable_access_key_authentication

feature_name: Use Centralized Identity and Authentication System — AAD Auth Required
notes: disable_local_auth=True enforces Entra ID as sole auth method for data plane

feature_name: Use Azure AD Managed Identities for Azure Resource Authentication
notes: namespace.identity present (SystemAssigned/UserAssigned) → PASS proxy; no identity → FAIL

feature_name: Use Azure AD Service Principals for Azure Resource Authentication
notes: disable_local_auth=True → Entra/RBAC enforced → SP assignments supported → PASS proxy

feature_name: Restrict Resource Access Based on Conditions — Conditional Access
notes: CA policies are Entra tenant-level configuration; not readable via ARM; always UNKNOWN

feature_name: Restrict the Exposure of Credential and Secrets — Use Azure Key Vault
notes: disable_local_auth=True → no SAS connection strings exposed → PASS; False → UNKNOWN (should use KV but not ARM-verifiable)

feature_name: Enable Threat Detection Capabilities — Microsoft Defender for Service Bus
notes: No Defender for Service Bus product in Defender for Cloud portfolio; always UNKNOWN with rationale

feature_name: Enable Logging for Azure Resources
notes: MonitorManagementClient.diagnostic_settings.list(ns.id); any enabled log category → PASS

feature_name: Ensure Regular Automated Backups — Azure Backup Service
notes: Azure Backup does not support Service Bus; geo-DR is availability failover not data backup → UNKNOWN

feature_name: Ensure Regular Automated Backups — Native Soft-Delete and Purge Protection
notes: Service Bus has no soft-delete or purge-protection for message data; entity config can be ARM-exported → UNKNOWN

feature_name: Use Only Approved Azure Services — Azure Policy Support
notes: Tags presence as proxy for policy governance; empty tags → FAIL

feature_name: Use Only Approved Applications in Virtual Machine — Defender for Cloud AAC
notes: PaaS — Adaptive Application Controls target VMs and Arc servers only

feature_name: Separate and Limit Highly Privileged/Administrative Users — Disable Local Admin
notes: disable_local_auth=False = SAS Manage-level keys active = local-admin equivalent; True → PASS

feature_name: Follow Just Enough Administration Principle — RBAC for Data Plane
notes: disable_local_auth=True = Entra/RBAC data-plane active; full JEA verification requires azure-mgmt-authorization

feature_name: Determine Access Process for Microsoft Support — Customer Lockbox
notes: SB in Lockbox supported services list (GA); enablement is subscription-level — per-namespace ARM check not available; always UNKNOWN with rationale

feature_name: EDR Solution
notes: PaaS — no customer-accessible compute; EDR agent deployment not supported

feature_name: Anti-Malware Solution
notes: PaaS — no customer OS layer; anti-malware not applicable

feature_name: Anti-Malware Solution Health Monitoring
notes: PaaS — ES-2 not applicable therefore ES-3 not applicable

feature_name: Azure Automation State Configuration
notes: PaaS — Automation DSC targets VMs and Arc machines; no customer OS layer

feature_name: Azure Policy Guest Configuration Agent
notes: PaaS — Guest Config agent requires customer-accessible guest OS

feature_name: Custom Container Images
notes: PaaS — Microsoft manages Service Bus runtime; no customer container images

feature_name: Custom VM Images
notes: PaaS — no customer-supplied VM images accepted by Service Bus

feature_name: Vulnerability Assessment using Microsoft Defender
notes: PaaS — no software stack to scan; Defender for Cloud has no VA product for Service Bus

feature_name: Azure Automation Update Management
notes: PaaS — Microsoft patches Service Bus infrastructure; Update Management targets VMs only
